steps.title: Identify the sentence structure and what punctuation is needed
steps.content: The sentence has two parts: an independent clause ending near "equations" and a supplementary adverb "though" followed by a second independent clause explaining how Hopper's career extended beyond equations. The blank must punctuate the boundary between the first clause, the supplement "though," and the explanatory clause that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — equations, though:: The comma separates the independent clause from the adverb "though," and the colon correctly introduces the explanatory clause that follows. ✓
  • Choice B — equations, though,: Creates a comma splice — a comma alone cannot join the two resulting independent clauses ("Hopper's…though" and "as…age"). ✗
  • Choice C — equations. Though,: The period fragments the thought, and starting the next sentence with "Though," illogically signals that what follows contradicts the previous sentence when it actually supports it. ✗
  • Choice D — equations though: No punctuation fuses two independent clauses into a run-on sentence. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
